mainTopic: (continues) מציאת שטרות וסימפון · מציאת פירות מפוזרים ויאוש שלא מדעת
overview: (1) The Mishnah lists various documents found in a bundle or container that must be returned, and the Gemara discusses the definition of a bundle (אגודה), the validity of a receipt written by the lender (סימפון), and the conditions under which a receipt is valid. (2) The Gemara begins the new perek discussing found scattered produce and coins, analyzing the concept of יאוש שלא מדעת (despair without knowledge) through various cases of lost items to determine if the owner despairs of recovery. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Mishnah lists various documents found in a bundle or container that must be returned, and the Gemara discusses the definition of a bundle (אגודה), the validity of a receipt written by the lender (סימפון), and the conditions under which a receipt is valid.

sugyaBoundaries.topic: מציאת פירות מפוזרים ויאוש שלא מדעת
sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Gemara begins the new perek discussing found scattered produce and coins, analyzing the concept of יאוש שלא מדעת (despair without knowledge) through various cases of lost items to determine if the owner despairs of recovery. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: Resolution: The court questions the witnesses about the payment
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating the court questions the witnesses about the payment.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by specifying that the בית דין questions the witnesses. This clarifies that the receipt's validity depends on their testimony regarding the payment.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The resolution hinges on active judicial inquiry. The witnesses are not merely present; they are interrogated to confirm whether the debt was actually paid. This shifts the burden of proof to the witnesses' testimony rather than the document's physical state.

keyTerms.meaning: Whether it is paid or not paid

phrases.aramaic: דְּשָׁיְילִינַן לְהוּ לְסָהֲדִי אִי פָּרוּעַ אִי לָא פָּרוּעַ.
phrases.english: We ask the witnesses: Was it uncovered? Was it not uncovered?

steps.triggerLanguage: דְּשָׁיְילִינַן לְהוּ לְסָהֲדִי אִי פָּרוּעַ אִי לָא פָּרוּעַ.

rashi.hebrew: דשיילינן להו - אם ראו הפרעון ואם לאו נאמן המלוה לומר שלא נכתב אלא להיות מוכן לכשיפרע:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the court questions the witnesses to determine if they saw the payment, and if they did not, the lender is believed when he claims the receipt was written only to be ready for a future payment.

steps.title: Challenge from a Baraita: A receipt with witnesses is valid
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution with a Baraita that says a receipt with witnesses is valid.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the previous resolution using a ברייתא. The ברייתא states that a receipt with witnesses is valid, seemingly contradicting the need for judicial questioning.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The challenge arises because the ברייתa implies validity based solely on the presence of witnesses. This suggests the receipt is valid even without the specific judicial inquiry mentioned in the resolution, creating a tension between the two sources.

keyTerms.meaning: A receipt that has witnesses on it

phrases.aramaic: תָּא שְׁמַע: סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים – כָּשֵׁר.
phrases.english: Come and hear: A simpon that has witnesses upon it – it is fit.
What are witnesses? Witnesses of completion.

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים – כָּשֵׁר. מַאי עֵדִים? עֵדֵי קִיּוּם.

rashi.hebrew: עדי קיום - שכתבו הנפק דבי דינא לא מקיימי ליה אלא א"כ פרע:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that עדי קיום are witnesses who testify that the court will not validate the receipt unless the debt is paid, thereby establishing the receipt's validity through their testimony of the court's procedure.

steps.title: Resolution: The witnesses are witnesses of ratification
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating the witnesses are witnesses of ratification.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by clarifying the role of the witnesses. They are identified as witnesses of ratification, not merely witnesses to the payment.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This distinction resolves the tension. The witnesses validate the document's authenticity and the transaction's legitimacy. Their presence ensures the receipt is not forged, which is a separate requirement from confirming the payment itself.

keyTerms.meaning: From the fact that it teaches in the latter part

phrases.english: Thus it is also logical, since the latter clause states:
And that which has no witnesses upon it — disqualified.
What is 'no witnesses upon it'?

phrases.aramaic: אִילֵּימָא דְּלֵיכָּא עִלָּוֵיהּ עֵדִים כְּלָל, צְרִיכָא לְמֵימַר דְּפָסוּל?
phrases.english: If I say that there are no witnesses upon it at all, is it necessary to state that it is…
But rather, not witnesses of completion.

steps.triggerLanguage: הָכִי נָמֵי מִסְתַּבְּרָא, מִדְקָתָנֵי סֵיפָא: וְשֶׁאֵין עָלָיו עֵדִים – פָּסוּל. מַאי ״אֵין עָלָיו עֵדִים״? אִילֵּימָא דְלֵיכָא עִלּוֹי עֵדִים כְּלָל, צְרִיכָא לְמֵימַר דְּפָסוּל?! אֶלָּא לָאו עֵדֵי קִיּוּם.

steps.title: Discussion of the Baraita: A receipt with witnesses is ratified by its signatories
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara discusses the Baraita that says a receipt with witnesses is ratified by its signatories.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא shifts to a direct discussion of the ברייתא's content. It examines the validity of receipts with witnesses and those without, depending on their origin.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The discussion distinguishes between receipts found with witnesses and those without. It introduces the concept that a receipt's validity can depend on whether it comes from a third party or after the signing of documents, setting up a detailed analysis.

keyTerms.meaning: A receipt that has witnesses on it
It is ratified by its signatories

phrases.aramaic: גּוּפָא. סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים – יִתְקַיֵּים בְּחוֹתְמָיו.
phrases.english: The main point. A document that has witnesses on it — it is valid by its signatures.

phrases.aramaic: אֵין עָלָיו עֵדִים וְיוֹצֵא מִתַּחַת יְדֵי שָׁלִישׁ,
phrases.english: There are no witnesses on it and it comes out from the hand of a third party,

phrases.aramaic: אוֹ שֶׁיּוֹצֵא לְאַחַר חִיתּוּם שְׁטָרוֹת – כָּשֵׁר.
phrases.english: Or that it comes out after the signing of documents — it is valid.

steps.triggerLanguage: גּוּפָא. סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים – יִתְקַיֵּים בְּחוֹתְמָיו. אֵין עָלָיו עֵדִים וְיוֹצֵא מִתַּחַת יְדֵי שָׁלִישׁ, אוֹ שֶׁיּוֹצֵא לְאַחַר חִיתּוּם שְׁטָרוֹת – כָּשֵׁר.

rashi.hebrew: סמפון שיש עליו עדים - בעדי קיום מוקמינן לה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase סמפון שיש עליו עדים refers to a receipt that is validated by witnesses who testify to its delivery (עדי קיום).

rashi.hebrew: יתקיים בחותמיו - ואפי' יוצא מתחת יד מלוה כשר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that יתקיים בחותמיו means the receipt is valid based on the signatures of the witnesses, even if it is produced by the lender himself.

rashi.hebrew: אין עליו עדי קיום - אבל עדים חתומין עליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that אין עליו עדים means the receipt lacks witnesses who testify to its delivery, though it still bears the signatures of witnesses on the document itself.

rashi.hebrew: ויוצא מתחת ידי שליש - שאין הלוה מוציאו ולא המלוה אלא שליש שביניהם נאמן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that ויוצא מתחת ידי שליש means the receipt is produced by a neutral third party, who is trusted because neither the borrower nor the lender is the one presenting it.

rashi.hebrew: או שיצא אחר חיתום שטרות - שיצא לפנינו כשהוא כתוב בשטר חוב אחר החתימה כשר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that או שיצא אחר חיתום שטרות means the receipt is valid if it is produced in court after the loan document has already been signed, proving it was written at the time of the loan.

steps.title: Explanation: A receipt from a third party is valid because the lender trusts the third party
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara explains that a receipt from a third party is valid because the lender trusts the third party.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא explains the reasoning behind the ברייתא's rulings. It clarifies why receipts from third parties or after signing are considered valid.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The explanation relies on the lender's trust in the third party and the assumption that the lender would not undermine his own document if the debt were unpaid. This provides the logical basis for accepting such receipts as valid proof of payment.

keyTerms.meaning: That emerges from the hand of a third party
That the lender trusted the third party
That emerges after the signing of the documents

phrases.aramaic: יוֹצֵא מִתַּחַת יְדֵי שָׁלִישׁ, דְּהָא הֵימְנֵיהּ מַלְוֶה לְשָׁלִישׁ.
phrases.english: He goes out from under the hand of a third party, for the lender trusted the third party.
He goes out after the signing of documents also,

phrases.aramaic: דְּאִי לָאו דִּפְרִיעַ לָא הֲוָה מַרַע לֵיהּ לִשְׁטָרֵיהּ.
phrases.english: For if not, he would not have harmed his document.

steps.triggerLanguage: יוֹצֵא מִתַּחַת יְדֵי שָׁלִישׁ, דְּהָא הֵימְנֵיהּ מַלְוֶה לְשָׁלִישׁ. יוֹצֵא לְאַחַר חִיתּוּם שְׁטָרוֹת נָמֵי, דְּאִי לָאו דִּפְרִיעַ לָא הֲוָה מַרַע לֵיהּ לִשְׁטָרֵיהּ.

rashi.hebrew: דהא הימניה מלוה לשליש - דעל כרחך אין כותב שובר אלא מלוה והוא מסרו ליד השליש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the lender's trust in the third party is necessary because the lender is the one who writes the receipt and gives it to the third party.

steps.title: Closing of the previous sugya
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara formally concludes the previous sugya with the standard closing formula.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא uses the standard closing formula to end the previous sugya. This marks a clear transition to a new topic.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This is a structural marker indicating the end of the discussion on receipts and witnesses. It signals that the following material will address a different halachic issue, specifically regarding found items.

keyTerms.meaning: We have returned to you (closing formula)

phrases.english: We return to you, two who hold

steps.title: Introduction of the new Mishnah
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara introduces the new Mishnah which lists items that belong to the finder and those that require proclamation.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא quotes the משנה which establishes the categories of found items. It distinguishes between items that belong to the finder and those requiring proclamation.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה lists various items like scattered produce, coins, and bundles. It outlines the differing opinions of רבי מאיר, רבי יהודה, and רבי שמעון בן אלעזר regarding the obligation to proclaim found items based on distinguishing marks.

keyTerms.meaning: Found items that belong to the finder

phrases.aramaic: מַתְנִי׳ אֵלּוּ מְצִיאוֹת שֶׁלּוֹ, וְאֵלּוּ חַיָּיב לְהַכְרִיז.
phrases.english: MISHNA: These are finds that are his, and these he is obligated to announce.
These are finds that are his:
He found scattered produce, scattered coins,
bundles in the public domain, and round cakes of pressed figs,
loaves of a baker,
strings of fish,
and cuts of meat,

phrases.aramaic: וְגִיזֵּי צֶמֶר הַלְּקוּחִין מִמְּדִינָתָן, וַאֲנִיצֵי פִשְׁתָּן,
phrases.english: and fleeces of wool taken from their place, and bundles of flax,
and strips of purple wool –
these are his,
words of Rabbi Meir.
Rabbi Yehuda says:
Anything that has a distinguishing mark –
he is obligated to announce. How so?

phrases.aramaic: מָצָא עִגּוּל וּבְתוֹכוֹ חֶרֶס, כִּכָּר וּבְתוֹכוֹ מָעוֹת.
phrases.english: He found a round cake and inside it a shard, a loaf and inside it coins.
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar says:

phrases.aramaic: כׇּל כְּלֵי אַנְפּוּרְיָא אֵין חַיָּיב לְהַכְרִיז.
phrases.english: All vessels of anpurya, one is not obligated to announce.

steps.triggerLanguage: מַתְנִי׳ אֵלּוּ מְצִיאוֹת שֶׁלּוֹ, וְאֵלּוּ חַיָּיב לְהַכְרִיז.

rashi.hebrew: מתני' אלו מציאות: מצא פירות מפוזרין - נתייאשו הבעלים מהן כדאמר בגמרא והפקר הן:
rashi.english: Mishna - Elu Metziot: If he found scattered produce - their owners have abandoned them; so they are ownerless, as it says in the gemara.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah's list of items belonging to the finder is based on the principle that the original owners have already ייאוש (despaired) of recovering them, rendering them הפקר (ownerless).

rashi.hebrew: מעות מפוזרות - הואיל ואין להם סימן ניכר איאושי מיאש והוו להו הפקר וזהו טעם כולם:
rashi.english: Scattered coins - Since they do not have a clear identifying mark, he abandons [them], and they become ownerless. And this is the reason for all of [these items].
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that scattered coins lack a סימן (identifying mark), leading to the owner's ייאוש and thus the item becomes הפקר, which is the underlying rationale for all items in this Mishnah.

rashi.hebrew: ברה"ר - שהכל דשין עלייהו ואם היה סימן נקשר עליהן הרי הוא נשחת:
rashi.english: In a public area - as everyone tramples them. And if they had an identifying mark, it is surely destroyed.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that items found in רשות הרבים (public domain) belong to the finder because any סימן (identifying mark) would inevitably be destroyed by the public trampling on them.

rashi.hebrew: של נחתום - כל ככרות הנחתומין שוין אבל ככרות של בעל הבית יש בהן סימן:
rashi.english: Of a baker - All baker's bread is the same, but bread [from the kitchen] of a homeowner has an identifying mark.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י distinguishes between a baker's loaves, which are uniform and lack a סימן, and a homeowner's loaves, which typically possess a distinguishing mark.

rashi.hebrew: ממדינתן - כמות שהן גזוזות כשאר כל גיזת המדינה לאפוקי הבאות מבית האומן כדקתני סיפא:
rashi.english: From their state - As they are, when they are shorn like all of the the shearings in the state. [This is] to exclude those that come from the craftsman's home, as it is learned in the lower part (the next mishnah).
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that wool shorn in its native province is uniform like all other wool from that region, excluding wool processed by a craftsman which might have a unique mark as discussed in the subsequent Mishnah.

rashi.hebrew: ולשון של ארגמן - צמר סרוק ומשוך כמין לשון וצבוע ארגמן ומצויין הן:
rashi.english: Strips (literally, tongues) of purple wool - Combed and pulled wool, then dyed purple; and they are common.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י describes the 'tongues of purple wool' as combed, pulled, and dyed wool that is common and therefore lacks a unique identifying mark.

tosafot.hebrew: מתני' אלו מציאות שלו: כריכות ברה"ר - בכריכות דוקא מפליג בין רה"ר לרה"י משום דלמ"ד (לקמן בבא מציעא כב:) סימן העשוי לידרס הוי סימן ומקום הוי סימן מיירי הכא דליכא סימן ומקום נמי לא הוי סימן ברה"ר משום דמנשתפי וברה"י הוי מקום סימן וחייב להכריז ומיירי דאשכח דרך הינוח וככרות של נחתום ועיגולי דבילה אין בהם סימן והוי שלו אף ברה"י ומיירי דמצא דרך נפילה ולכך לא הוי מקום סימן וא"ת אמאי נקט כריכות דאשכח דרך הינוח טפי משאר דאיירי דרך נפילה וי"ל דכריכות איידי דיקירי אינון לא הוה שיפלו שלא מדעת אלא כשעומד לפוש מניח לארץ ושכחם שם וככרות של בעה"ב יש להן סימן דבעלי בתים רגילים לעשות סימן בככרותיהן ולכך אפילו ברשות הרבים חייב להכריז דסימן העשוי לידרס הוי סימן וציבורי מעות ופירות דהוי מקום סימן אף ברה"ר כדאמר לקמן משום דלא שייך בהו מנשתפי ואם תאמר כריכות ברה"ר דמיירי דרך הינוח היאך לוקחם הא אמרינן לקמן ספק הינוח לא יטול וכל שכן ודאי הינוח וי"ל דה"מ כשמצא במקום מוצנע ומשתמר דלא יטול אם אין בו סימן ואם יש בו סימן נוטל ומכריז ולמ"ד (לקמן בבא מציעא כב:) סימן העשוי לידרס לא הוי סימן ומקום נמי לא הוי סימן מיירי בכריכות שיש בהם סימן ולכך ברה"י מכריז וככרות של נחתום ושאר דמתניתין לית בהו סימן ולכך אף ברה"י הם שלו וככרות של בעה"ב שיש בהם סימן חייב להכריז אף ברה"ר דאין עשוי לידרס משום דאין מעבירין על האוכלין וכולה מתני' מיירי בין דרך נפילה בין דרך הינוח וא"ת כריכות אי דרך נפילה היא אם כן אינו יודע היכן נפלו וברשות היחיד אמאי צריך להכריז הא מתייאש שסבור ברה"ר נפיל וי"ל דאע"פ שאינו יודע לכוין המקום יודע הוא אם נפל ברשות הרבים או ברשות היחיד:
tosafot.english: Mishnah - Elu Metziot Shelo: Bundles in a public area - It is specifically with bundles that it distinguishes between a public area and a private area: As according to the one that holds (later, on Bava Metzia 22b) that an identifying mark that is prone to be trampled is [considered] an identifying mark, and that a place is an identifying mark - it is speaking here when there is no identifying mark; and the place is also not an identifying mark in a public area, because they roll around. But the place is an identifying mark in a private area - such that one would be obligated to announce [them when they are found there]. And that is speaking about when it is found in the manner of being placed down (intentionally). But bread of a baker and round cakes of figs do not have an identifying mark, so they are [the finder's] even in a private area. And that is speaking about when he found it in the manner of [something] being dropped - hence the place is not an identifying mark. And if you will say, "Why does it mention bundles, in the manner of being placed down, more than the other [cases] - about which it is speaking in the manner of being dropped" - one can say that because the bundles are expensive, they would not have been dropped without his awareness (he would be more mindful about them). Rather, [the case would be] that when he stopped to rest, he placed them on the ground and forgot them there. However, the bread [from the kitchen] of a homeowner has an identifying mark, for homeowners are accustomed to make an identifying mark on their bread. Hence even [if they were found] in a public area, one is obligated to announce [them] - for an identifying mark that is prone to be trampled is [considered] an identifying mark. And [the reason] that the place is an identifying mark with piles of fruit or piles of money even in a public place - as it says later on - is because rolling around is not applicable to them. And if you will say, "How can [finders] take bundles in a public place when it is speaking about [them being found in] the manner of being placed down - behold, we say later that one may not take [an object when there is] a doubt that it was placed down, and all the more so [when it is] certain" - one can say that those words (that case) is when he finds [it] in a hidden and guarded place, such that he should not take it if there is no identifying mark; and if there is an identifying mark, he takes [it] and announces [it]. And according to the one that holds (later, on Bava Metzia 22b) that an identifying mark that is prone to be trampled is not [considered] an identifying mark, and that a place is also not an identifying mark - it is speaking about bundles that have an identifying mark. And hence one announces it [when it was found] in a private area. But the bread of the baker and the rest of [the cases in] our mishnah do not have an identifying mark. And hence they are his, even [if they were found] in a private area. However, he is obligated to announce the bread [from the kitchen] of a homeowner that has an identifying mark, even [when found] in a public place - as it is not something that is prone to be trampled, since we do not pass by food [without picking it up]. And our entire mishnah is speaking about whether [the object] was [found] in the manner of being dropped or whether it was in the manner of being placed down. And if you will say, "If the bundles were in the manner of being dropped, hence [the one who dropped them] does not know where they are; so why does he need to announce [them] - behold [the owner has abandoned them, since he believes that they fell in the public area" - one can say that even though he is not able to determine the place [where he dropped it], he knows whether it fell in a public area or a private area.
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות analyzes why the Mishnah specifically distinguishes between רשות הרבים and רשות היחיד for bundles, explaining that in a public domain the location is not a סימן due to items rolling around, whereas in a private domain the location serves as a סימן if the item was placed there intentionally (דרך הינוח).

steps.title: Defining the quantity of scattered produce
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how much produce constitutes 'scattered produce' and cites Rabbi Yitzchak's definition.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks a clarifying question about the משנה's term 'scattered produce'. It provides רבי יצחק's quantitative definition to resolve the ambiguity.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The question seeks to define the threshold of 'scattered produce' by asking 'how much?'. The answer cites רבי יצחק's ruling of one kav in four cubits, establishing a specific measure for determining whether produce is considered scattered or not.

phrases.english: Gemara: Found scattered produce. And how much?

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַבִּי יִצְחָק: קַב בְּאַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת.
phrases.english: Rabbi Yitzchak said: A kav in four cubits.

steps.triggerLanguage: מָצָא פֵּירוֹת מְפוּזָּרִין. וְכַמָּה? אָמַר רַבִּי יִצְחָק: קַב בְּאַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת.

rashi.hebrew: גמ' וכמה - חשוב פיזור:
rashi.english: Gemara: And how much - is [still] considered scattered?
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's question 'וכמה' is asking for the quantitative threshold at which produce is still considered 'פיזור' (scattered).

rashi.hebrew: קב - מפוזר בארבע אמות אבל בג' אמות לא הוי פיזור וטעמא מפרש ואזיל:
rashi.english: A kav - scattered in four [by four] cubits; but in three [by three] cubits, it is not [considered] scattered. And the reason will be explained as we go.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that רבי יצחק defines 'פיזור' as a קב scattered over four אמות, whereas if it were only three אמות it would not be considered scattered, and notes that the underlying reason for this measurement will be explained later in the Gemara.

tosafot.hebrew: וכמה א"ר יצחק קב בארבע אמות - צריך לומר דרבי יצחק גופיה בעי וכמה דאי גמרא בעי מאי בעי וכמה לוקי דרך נפילה ואפילו טובא כדפריך גמ' בתר הכי אלא מדברי רבי יצחק הוא ורבי יצחק לא בעי לאוקמי דרך נפילה ואפילו טובא משום דסבר כאביי דיאוש שלא מדעת לא הוי יאוש אלא מוקי לה במכנשתא דבי דרי שהניחם שם מדעת וכה"ג איכא בפירקין (דף ל:) דבעי ולעולם אמר רב יהודה כו' דצריך לומר דרב יהודה אמר ולעולם וכן בפרק לא יחפור (ב"ב דף כב: ושם ד"ה וכמה) גבי מרחיקין מן החלונות דקאמר וכמה אמר רב ייבא כמלוא רוחב חלון:
tosafot.english: And how much? Rabbi Yitzchak says, "One kav in four [by four] cubits" - and it must be said that Rabbi Yitzchak himself inquired, "And how much?" For if the gemara inquired, "And how much," let it establish [the case as being in] the manner of being dropped, and even more [than a kav] - as the gemara challenges after this. Rather [the question] is from the words of Rabbi Yitzchak. And Rabbi Yitzchak may not establish [the case to be] in the manner of being dropped and even more, because he holds - like Abbaye - that abandonment without intention is not [considered] abandonment. Rather, he establishes it to be in the gathering [of grain] on the threshing floor, such that he left them there intentionally. And likewise in this way is it found in our chapter (Bava Metzia 30b) that when it inquires, "And forever? Rav Yehuda [answers], etc." - it must be said that Rav Yehudah said, "And forever?" And likewise in the chapter [entitled] Lo Yachpor (Bava Batra 22b, and Tosafot there, s.v. and how much) concerning distancing from windows, such that it says, "And how much? Rav Yeiva said, 'The full width of the window.'"
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות argues that the question 'וכמה' must be attributed to רבי יצחק himself rather than the Gemara, because if the Gemara asked it, it would have established the case as produce dropped unintentionally (which could be a larger quantity), but רבי יצחק holds like אביי that יאוש שלא מדעת is not valid יאוש, so he defines the quantity based on produce intentionally left on the threshing floor.

meforshim.interplaySummary: תוספות challenges the assumption that the Gemara is asking the question, arguing instead that רבי יצחק is asking it himself to avoid a contradiction with his halachic stance on יאוש שלא מדעת, whereas רש״י simply treats it as a standard Gemara inquiry into the definition of פיזור.

steps.title: Challenge based on manner of scattering
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges Rabbi Yitzchak's definition by questioning whether the produce fell accidentally or was placed intentionally.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a difficulty against the definition of scattered produce by considering two scenarios: accidental falling vs. intentional placement.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא argues that if the produce fell accidentally, even a large amount should belong to the finder, and if it was placed intentionally, even a small amount should not. This challenges the fixed quantity definition.

phrases.english: How is it similar?

phrases.aramaic: אִי דֶּרֶךְ נְפִילָה – אֲפִילּוּ טוּבָא נָמֵי!
phrases.english: If it is the way of falling — even much also!
And if it is the way of placing —
Even less than this also not!

steps.triggerLanguage: הֵיכִי דָּמֵי? אִי דֶּרֶךְ נְפִילָה – אֲפִילּוּ טוּבָא נָמֵי! וְאִי דֶּרֶךְ הִינּוּחַ – אֲפִילּוּ בְּצִיר מֵהָכִי נָמֵי לָא!

rashi.hebrew: אי דרך נפילה - אם מצאם דרך נפילה שיש לדעת שלא הונחו שם מדעת אלא נפלו:
rashi.english: If in a manner of falling - If he found them in the manner of being dropped; [such] that it can be ascertained that they were not placed there internationally, but rather fell.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that דרך נפילה refers to produce that clearly fell accidentally rather than being intentionally placed.

rashi.hebrew: אפילו טובא - מקב נמי דכיון דאין בהן סימן איאושי מיאש:
rashi.english: Even greater - than a kav as well; as there is no sign, [the owner] abandoned [them].
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that if produce fell accidentally, the owner יאוש even for a large amount like a מקב because there is no סימן.

rashi.hebrew: ואי דרך הינוח וכו' - עתיד לחזור וליטלן:
rashi.english: And if in a manner of placement, etc. - he will return and take them in the future.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that if produce was intentionally placed, the owner will return to collect it, so even a small amount remains his.

steps.title: Resolution: wheat kernels on a threshing floor
steps.stepSummary: Rav Ukva bar Chama resolves the difficulty by specifying the case as wheat kernels left on a threshing floor, where effort determines despair.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבּ אֻקְבָּא בַּר חֲמָא provides a resolution by narrowing the context to wheat kernels on a threshing floor, where the effort to gather them determines whether the owner despairs.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The resolution explains that one קַב in four אַמּוֹת represents a threshold of effort. If gathering requires too much effort, the owner despairs; if less, he does not. This bridges the gap between accidental falling and intentional placement.

keyTerms.meaning: Gathering of grain on a threshing floor

phrases.english: Rav Ukva bar Ḥama said:
We are dealing with kernels of wheat that remained during the gathering of grain on…
For kernels scattered with a dispersal ratio of one kav in an area of four…

phrases.aramaic: לָא טָרַח אִינִישׁ וְלָא הָדַר אָתֵי וְשָׁקֵיל לְהוּ,
phrases.english: a person does not exert himself and does not return and take them.
Therefore, he renounces his ownership of them and one who finds the kernels may keep…
For kernels scattered in an area smaller than that,
the owner exerts himself and returns and takes them.
And therefore, he does not renounces his ownership of them.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב עוּקְבָא בַּר חָמָא: בְּמַכְנַשְׁתָּא דְּבֵי דָרֵי עָסְקִינַן.

rashi.hebrew: במכנשתא דבי דרי - בשעת אסיפת גרנות וכאן דשן בעליהן ונשאו את העיקר ונותרו אלו:
rashi.english: The gathering on the threshing floor - At the time of the gathering of the threshing floors; and their owners thresh [the grain] and carry off the main part and leave these over.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that במכנשתא דבי דרי refers to the time of gathering from the threshing floor, where the owners have already taken the main crop and left these kernels behind.

rashi.hebrew: נפיש טרחייהו - לקבצן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that נפיש טרחייהו specifically refers to the exertion required to collect the scattered kernels.

rashi.hebrew: בציר מהכי - אם היה פזורן בפחות מכן:
rashi.english: Smaller than that - If their scattering was in [an area] less than this.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that בציר מהכי refers to a scenario where the kernels are scattered over a smaller area, requiring less effort to gather.

steps.title: Dilemma: half-kav in two cubits
steps.stepSummary: Rabbi Yirmeya raises a dilemma about whether a half-kav in two cubits belongs to the finder, questioning the underlying reason for despair.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה poses a dilemma regarding a smaller quantity and area, questioning whether despair is based on effort or value.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The dilemma explores two possible reasons for despair in the original case: excessive effort or low value. If effort is the reason, a smaller amount in a smaller area might not cause despair. If value is the reason, it might still cause despair.

phrases.aramaic: בָּעֵי רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה: חֲצִי קַב בִּשְׁתֵּי אַמּוֹת, מַהוּ?
phrases.english: Rabbi Yirmeya asks: A half-kav in two cubits, what is the law?
A kav in four cubits,
What is the reason –
Because their labor is great.
A half-kav in two cubits,
Since their labor is not great –
He does not renounce them. Or perhaps:
Because they are not significant,
And a half-kav in two cubits,
Since they are not significant – he renounces them.

steps.triggerLanguage: בָּעֵי רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה: חֲצִי קַב בִּשְׁתֵּי אַמּוֹת, מַהוּ?

rashi.hebrew: משום דלא חשיבי - עליה קב פירות לטרוח עליהן טורח קיבוץ של ארבע אמות:
rashi.english: Because they are not of significant [value] - to him, the kav of produce, to exert himself for them with the exertion of gathering [them] over four [by four] cubits.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's alternative reason for despair is that the produce is not considered significant enough to justify the effort of gathering it over an area of four cubits.

tosafot.hebrew: חצי קב בשתי אמות מהו - תימה הלא קב בארבע אמות הוי נמי חצי קב בשתי אמות ומאי קמבעיא ליה וי"ל כיון דליכא אלא חצי קב ילקוט כיון שאין לו טורח לגמור אבל קב בארבע אמות יש לו טורח ללקוט הכל ואין מלקט כלל אי נמי קב בארבע אמות מרובעין הוי חצי קב בשתי אמות רוחב וארבע אמות אורך והכא בעי חצי קב בב' אמות על ב' אמות:
tosafot.english: A half-kav in an area of two cubits, what is [the law] - It is a wonder: Is a kav in four cubits not also a half-kav in two cubits (is the ratio not the same); so what is he inquiring? And one can say that since there is only a half-kav, he will take [them] since there is no exertion [beyond this] to finish. But [with] a kav in four cubits, since there is exertion to take all of it, he does not take [them] at all. Or also (another answer), a kav in four by four cubits is [the same as] half a kav in two cubits width by four cubits length, whereas here he is inquiring about two cubits by two cubits.
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the difficulty that a קב in four cubits is proportionally the same as a חצי קב in two cubits, and offers two resolutions: either the owner will gather a smaller amount due to less effort, or the original case refers to a square area while the dilemma refers to a different shape.

steps.title: Dilemma: two kav in eight cubits
steps.stepSummary: Rabbi Yirmeya raises a second dilemma about a larger quantity and area, further testing the effort vs. value distinction.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה poses another dilemma with a larger quantity and area, continuing to test whether despair is based on effort or value.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This dilemma flips the variables: more produce in a larger area. If effort is the reason, despair should be even stronger. If value is the reason, the larger amount might be significant enough to prevent despair.

phrases.english: Two kav in eight cubits — what is the law?
One kav in four cubits,
What is the reason —
Because their labor is great,
And all the more so two kav in eight cubits,
Since their labor is greater —
He renounces them. Or perhaps:
Because they are not significant,
And two kav in eight cubits,
Since they are significant —
He does not renounce them.

steps.title: Dilemma: sesame seeds
steps.stepSummary: Rabbi Yirmeya raises a third dilemma involving sesame seeds, which are more valuable and harder to gather, further testing the criteria.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה poses a dilemma involving sesame seeds, which are more valuable and harder to gather, continuing to test the effort vs. value distinction.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Sesame seeds are more valuable (potentially preventing despair) but harder to gather (potentially causing despair). This dilemma isolates the value vs. effort factors by changing the type of produce.

phrases.english: A kav of sesame seeds in four cubits — what is the law?
A kav in four cubits, what is the reason?
Because they are not significant,
And sesame seeds, since they are significant —
He does not renounce them. Or perhaps:
Because their labor is great,
And all the more so sesame seeds,

phrases.aramaic: כֵּיוָן דִּנְפִישׁ טִרְחַיְיהוּ טְפֵי – מַפְקַר לְהוּ.
phrases.english: Since their labor is even greater — he renounces them.

rashi.hebrew: שומשמין - דקין מאד ויש טורח בקיבוצן יותר מחטין אבל דמיהן יקרין:
rashi.english: Sesame [seeds] - are very small, so there is more exertion than [with] wheat; but their value is high.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that שומשמין are very small, making their collection more labor-intensive than wheat, but their monetary value is higher.

steps.title: Dilemma: dates and pomegranates
steps.stepSummary: Rabbi Yirmeya raises a final dilemma involving dates and pomegranates, which are less valuable and easier to gather.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּי יִרְמְיָה poses a final dilemma involving dates and pomegranates, which are less valuable and easier to gather, and the גמרא concludes with a תֵּיקוּ.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Dates and pomegranates are less valuable (potentially causing despair) but easier to gather (potentially preventing despair). The גמרא concludes that all these dilemmas remain unresolved (תֵּיקוּ).

phrases.english: A kav of dates in four cubits,
A kav of pomegranates in four cubits, what is the law?
A kav in four cubits, what is the reason?
Because they are not significant.
A kav of dates in four cubits,
A kav of pomegranates in four cubits also,
Since they are not significant –
He renounces them. Or perhaps:
Because their labor is great,
And a kav of dates in four cubits,
And a kav of pomegranates in four cubits,
Since their labor is not great –
He does not renounce them. What? Let it stand unresolved.

steps.triggerLanguage: קַב תַּמְרֵי בְּאַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת, קַב רִמּוֹנֵי בְּאַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת, מַהוּ?

rashi.hebrew: תמרים ורמונין - גסים הן ואין טורח בקבוצן:
rashi.english: Dates and pomegranates - are large, and there is no exertion in gathering them.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that dates and pomegranates are considered coarse items that require no significant effort to gather.

steps.title: Introduction of the dispute on יאוש שלא מדעת
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara introduces a dispute between Abaye and Rava regarding whether despair without knowledge constitutes valid despair.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא transitions to a new topic, introducing a dispute between אַבָּיֵי and רַבָּא about the validity of despair without knowledge.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This is a new sugya opening with a standard 'it was stated' formula, presenting two opposing views on a fundamental halachic concept: whether despair without knowledge is valid.

phrases.english: It was stated: Despair without knowledge,
Abaye says: It is not despair.
And Rava says: It is despair.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִיתְּמַר: יֵאוּשׁ שֶׁלֹּא מִדַּעַת, אַבָּיֵי אָמַר: לָא הָוֵי יֵאוּשׁ. וְרָבָא אָמַר: הָוֵי יֵאוּשׁ.
